name: Los Riscos de Yebenes - circular trail run by windmills
name_original: Los Riscos de Yébenes – trail run circular por molinos de viento
region_intro: Jogging routes around Los Yébenes traverse a diverse landscape within the Montes de Toledo, characterized by Mediterranean forest ecosystems, expansive plains, and quartzite ridges. The region's terrain includes well-preserved holm oak and cork oak forests, offering shaded paths. Extensive "rañas" provide open plains, while striking quartzite and slate ridges present varied inclines. This natural setting offers a range of running experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. Best jogging routes around Los Yébenes The most popular jogging route is Los Riscos de Yebenes - circular trail run by windmills, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route winds through the highest parts of the sierra, offering panoramic views and passing by historical sites. Another top favourite among local runners is The Highlights of Orgaz – circular trail run through the Umbría del Madroñal, a moderate 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This circular route leads through the Umbría del Madroñal, showcasing varied natural scenery. Local runners also love the Los Yébenes – San Huberto Arch, Los Yébenes loop from Los Yébenes, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through the area around San Huberto Arch, often completed in about 42 minutes. Jogging around Los Yébenes is defined by Mediterranean forests, expansive plains, and quartzite ridges. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Los Yébenes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 113 runners have used komoot to explore Los Yébenes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Los Yébenes?
A: Los Yébenes offers a good selection of running routes, with nearly 20 trails cataloged on komoot. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from Mediterranean forests to expansive plains and quartzite ridges, providing varied experiences for runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Los Yébenes?
A: The jogging routes in Los Yébenes feature a diverse terrain. You'll encounter well-preserved Mediterranean forests with holm oaks and cork oaks, offering shaded paths. There are also extensive 'rañas' (plains) and striking quartzite and slate ridges, providing varied inclines and scenic vistas. This mix ensures a dynamic running experience.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Los Yébenes?
A: While most routes are moderate, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38145868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Yébenes – San Huberto Arch, Los Yébenes loop from Los Yébenes&lt;/a&gt; is a good option for a shorter, moderate run at 6.2 km. Additionally, the Dehesa Boyal municipal estate offers routes like La Traviesa de las Corzas (3.7 km), which are suitable for various activities including easier jogging.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Los Yébenes?
A: The running routes in Los Yébenes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 113 runners have explored the area, often praising the varied terrain and the natural beauty of the Montes de Toledo.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Los Yébenes?
A: Yes, Los Yébenes features several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021455241/los-riscos-de-yebenes-circular-trail-run-by-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Riscos de Yebenes - circular trail run by windmills&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.4 km long and offers panoramic views. Another great option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021457935/the-highlights-of-orgaz-circular-trail-run-through-the-umbria-del-madronal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Highlights of Orgaz – circular trail run through the Umbría del Madroñal&lt;/a&gt;, covering 14.9 km.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while running in Los Yébenes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021455241/los-riscos-de-yebenes-circular-trail-run-by-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Riscos de Yebenes trail&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent panoramic views of the Algodor Valley and passes by historical sites like La Chorrera and El Montón de Trigo. You can also spot the iconic Windmills of the Crestería Molinera, including the Tío Zacarías Mill. For elevated views, consider routes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2907392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de los Moros Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2699703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Amor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or cultural attractions?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021455241/los-riscos-de-yebenes-circular-trail-run-by-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Riscos de Yebenes trail&lt;/a&gt; winds through the sierra and passes by ancient Bronze Age settlements like La Chorrera and El Montón de Trigo. Additionally, the region is home to several castles, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Orgaz&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3792122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Negras Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored in the vicinity.

Q: What is the best season for running in Los Yébenes?
A: Los Yébenes, with its Mediterranean climate, is generally pleasant for running during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. The well-preserved Mediterranean forests offer shaded paths, which can be particularly welcome during warmer months. The 'bellowing of the deer' (la berrea) in September also makes autumn a unique time to visit.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in Los Yébenes?
A: For longer runs, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021455241/los-riscos-de-yebenes-circular-trail-run-by-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Riscos de Yebenes - circular trail run by windmills&lt;/a&gt; covers 15.4 km. Another good option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021457935/the-highlights-of-orgaz-circular-trail-run-through-the-umbria-del-madronal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Highlights of Orgaz – circular trail run through the Umbría del Madroñal&lt;/a&gt; at 14.9 km. The Dehesa Boyal also features the Los Cejos del Río route, which is 13 km long.

Q: Can I find routes that are less crowded for a more peaceful run?
A: While specific crowd levels vary, exploring routes within the 1500-hectare Dehesa Boyal municipal estate, such as La Traviesa de las Corzas, La Trocha de Sopié, or Los Cejos del Río, might offer a more secluded experience. These routes wind through native forest, providing a tranquil setting away from more popular spots.

Q: Are there any running routes near the Algodor River?
A: Yes, you can enjoy an easy 20.1-kilometer circular route through the fields of Los Yébenes that leads to the Algodor River. This route offers a more relaxed running experience and provides views of the river valley.
tips: The Molino del Tío Zacarías, located in the Sierra de los Yébenes (Toledo), near the municipality of Orgaz, is one of the most emblematic windmills in the area and has been declared a Site of Cultural Interest (BIC) as part of the region's milling complex.

The path of the Yébenes cliffs runs through a beautiful landscape on the outskirts of the town where you will find several viewpoints that offer beautiful views of the town and this entire area of the interior of Toledo.

waypoints.name: Trail along the cliffs of Yébenes
